steps.title: Find the shared concern
steps.content: Both texts focus on whether studies of nutrient transfer can rule out non-CMN pathways. Text 1 says Chen's study lacked the barrier step, and Text 2 says even Alvarez's barriers could allow another pathway through liquids.

steps.title: Rule out unshared questions
steps.content: Choice A asks about the availability of a barrier material, which Text 1 does not address. Choice C would split the authors' views about Alvarez's design. Choice D raises root-to-root and CMN transfer together, which neither text directly settles.

steps.title: Choose Chen's pathway issue
steps.content: Both authors would point to the same conclusion about Chen's study: it did not effectively exclude nutrient pathways other than a CMN. The answer is B.
